channel.title: Drafts on Betzalel's writings
channel.description: Recent content in Drafts on Betzalel's writings

item.title: AI as progression
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • it’s just the loom all over again
  • are the movies right?
  • every invention has its ups and downs (good and bad)
  • our work is going to be needed elsewhere (space navigation?)
  • another path: the cyberpunk world of 2077
  • the choice is up to us
  • https://youtu.be/09y65-vH2Mk

item.title: can you make a one-man army
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • Cognitive limitations
  • Is it just a

item.title: doing evil for the sake of good
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • reference code Geass
  • reference invincibles (“either you’re the good guy or you’re the one who saves the world” quote)
  • I’m not saying what Hitler did wasn’t bad but because of that Israel is a country and the Jews are safer than ever
  • looking at Good and evil as polarity (reference hermetic philosophy: the kybalion)
  • you can’t just have good because then there would be nothing everything would be static like an image if we want to progress there has to be evil whether we like it or not there has to be conflict there has to be wars.
  • reference Nietzsche’s beyond Good and evil book

item.title: is democracy the best we can do
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • is democracy better than all our other options?
  • looking at empire’s as a mechanical structure and not only in the voice of empathy
  • class based hierarchy
  • the star trooper republic: if you contribute to your country (eg. Service in the army or National) you get to vote
  • the Machiavellian way:
  • alternative option a mix of empire and democracy
  • what we will have to sacrifice and if it’s worth it or will it make us stronger?

item.title: music as an influence for emotion
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • depressed songs can make you depressed
  • music as a virus (reference snow crash)
  • why is music that makes you feel bad so addicting?
  • you are what you listen to

item.title: Psyops and conspiracies
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • what’s the difference

item.title: racism as a defense mechanism
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • the neurology behind it (disable racism disable belief in God)
  • about racism death by other tribes becomes more of a possibility
  • tribalism is biological
  • the progress against the firewall

item.title: Sense of humor and of mindsets
Spinoza's ethics and modern dating
item.description: About a year ago I watched a video on Spinoza’s ethics (spicificly on love) and I saw it as world shattering, fast forward to today (13/04/2026) and I recalled what I watched, and then it hit me. We don’t like to admit it but we unconsciously follow that system when it comes to dating. But before we get into the correlation I’m making here, what does Spinoza actually have to about love. I will be referring to spicificly ethics part IV mostly

item.title: Terrorism and extremism
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • the cause.

item.title: The broken school system
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • what’s broken, and was it done on purpose?
  • University style schooling system
  • we need more questions than rules
  • do we need stupid (lesser in intelligence) people anymore?
  • the breeding of mindless drones just made to do biddings without questioning
  • we need a system that breeds “thinkers with the ability to do” not ’thinkers’ nor ‘doers’ but a mixture of both
  • can this new system fix depression? (Well also reference the essay I wrote on depression)

item.title: The broken society we don't know we live in
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • why would someone break a perfectly working system on purpose? If it was working perfectly fine that is
  • consumerism
  • the temporary effects of prescribed medication and why there has to be a better way of dealing with those problems
  • modernity and how it leads to nihilism
  • the age of the last man (and woman specifically self-hatred)
  • can we fix what was made to be broken?
  • the dating system (push for the old school shidduch system or even arranged marriages.)
  • A woman is not a car (specifically hookup culture)
  • the mouse utopia experiment
  • an Israeli’s perspective on American ideology / society and how it’s shaping Israel (reference Aldus Huxley’s introduction to brave New world: “First America then the rest of the world follows”.)
  • Wokeism, advice for teens and the dangers of casual relationships

item.title: the collective unconscious we call the internet
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • a physical (more interactive?) version of Carl Jung’s collective unconscious
  • maybe talk about how echo Chambers are somewhat like being in loop of some sort?
  • how is this going to impact the future?

item.title: the days of Messiah
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • the taboo index (reference sword art online)
  • Judaism was just a stepping stone

item.title: The DSM, Identity and the spectrum
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • https://youtu.be/6gUtpptU5xE
  • the holy bible of identity (the DSM “Manual”)

item.title: the gender war
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • https://youtu.be/2uDEM9R5E2Y
  • https://youtu.be/8OzGilusxtA
  • we fear what we don’t have: men with their strength, women with their beauty

item.title: the god variable
item.description:
Bullet points;

  • one less variable makes the calculation more predictable but less accurate
  • we refuse to tell ourselves we don’t know something that is beyond our control / imagination

item.title: The hell you don't want to leave
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • repeating an action to death because it’s easier then the alternative. This also explains why people “stay in their lane” it’s comfortable and eazy, the “doing something else” or switching a lane is a disruption to the norm and scary. The problem is it’s sometimes necessary—especially if you have any bad habbits.

item.title: The industries that control are every move
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • maybe it’s not all politics (the left as the victim of big corporations wanting you to believe stupid things so that they make money)
  • the 1954 rat experiment (where there was a rat that they hooked electrodes to the stimulus part of the rat’s brain and it kept on pressing the button to be stimulated even when there was food right next to it) somehow connect that to the porn industry and tobacco industry maybe even media industry definitely Coca-Cola
  • the Disney protocol: hooking us on a feeling to make us want to chase something that doesn’t exist also they are destroying specifically families with hidden messages and the fantasy of true love and love at first sight that they’ve pushed and now delving deeper into the feminism part where they’re basically programming young women’s minds to not want to have children and be independent (which kind of just sounds sad and lonely) and labeling it as a choice when in reality you’re literally programmed to want to have children (whether you’re male or female) whether you like it or not and then wonder why they don’t feel fulfilled.
  • inside the company’s and the rituals they make everyone do
  • is the water really tuning the frogs gay? (Investigate what there putting in the water and if Alex Jones was right)
  • “just think of the military!”: the military cannot repair their systems because “they don’t have the right to repair them”. Which puts actual lives at stake. (Contract financing)
  • big tech addicting our children to porn, social media and gambling.
  • “you will own nothing and be happy”
  • what are they putting in our food? Connecting the food industry to making cheap food that gives us problems and the pharmaceutical companies making Bank off of that.
  • https://youtu.be/ULFv8ZLitRU: consumerism in the food industry
  • how porn controls you: step one you counter it on a whim maybe curiosity or a friend shows it to you, step 2 you come back again after you’ve seen something that was quite esoteric wanted to double-check maybe, step 3 repeat step 2. after you’ve repeated all these steps you’ll start to realize you have quite a love hate relationship with the product which makes you keep on coming back or in other words an addiction, and then it ends up consuming you it becomes the only thing you can think of the only thing you want you become obsessed. A byproduct of this addiction I that it comes with another one on that some might argue is far worse.. masturbation: it feels great, calms you down, and “natural”. The problem is it also drains your energy your even when you wake up in the morning and sometimes you sleep through your alarms too. it also makes you terribly awkward not only that a waste of time (this one goes to both addictions) you feel empty after you’re done and not only that sure you had some nice instant pleasure but now what no reward—just a feeling and a quite disappointing feeling after that high, the problem is that high brings you back each time ..and the pretty women on the screen or if you’re a smut reader the enticing/exciting (I don’t read smut novels so I’m kind of guessing on a whim here) story. The question now is how do you get over it? As much as everyone hates it the red pillers (white pillers?) might have a point. Specifically speaking, discipline; not doing something you really really want to do.and no it not a walk in the park either, it is gruesome and agonizing. but only for a week or two, after that it get so much easier you start to forget that part of that reality in the first place. Another thing that might actually help is being delusional (seriously); believing that part of the internet doesn’t exist you can trick your brain into doing that you may never look back. You start to see more in the world you start to realize how much more there is to living then just one simple act, you also gain more energy and feel stronger feel like you can actually do things in this world—things that no man else can do. But in the end it’s up to you do you really want that instant pleasure at the cost of something greater? Or do you want to jump out of that box and run to the finish line whilst everyone else is limping.

item.title: the meaning bucket list
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • meaning will always change one day will be we got to fight a war against ex-person and win it and that could be a great powerful anchor for meaning but then what happens when that ends what is the next one what happens after let’s just say for example hypothetically everybody gets digitized into a world what would be the meaning then it would probably be a little bit nihilistic people can do whatever they want so then we have to find some sort of meaning like maybe search for it other life in this universe this is a bit of an extreme situation but is it’s always a possibility that will happen in the future not the point though we have to make a step by step bucket list we could call it even of what to search for in each era so that we don’t end up in the nihilistic age of modernity that we are in now

item.title: the modern gods
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • materialism / naturalism
  • the materialistic gods and our unconscious rituals we perform to them. Specifically talking about three of them maybe even four sex money and the crowd which I would also add Fame as one of but I think that one would be more connected with the crowd
  • God is dead and we have killed him
  • is there still a reason to believe in the ancient G-d? Not talking about what the Greeks did which was basically turn materialistic things into gods but we’re talking about the Hebrew God or the one true G-d
  • giving God a physical form makes it lose its meaning
  • https://youtu.be/Gzao9UDEurY idolizing the one / the religion of the one true love
  • original quote: “Without a god to serve we end up idolizing something else as a god.” And then start explaining why monotheistic religions or progressing Humanity forward not holding them back because would end up idolizing other objects and people as gods we would do rituals for them and that will make a slaves to materialistic things and one of the purposes of religion was answering the question “can you be asleep to nothing?” The answer was no so monotheistic religion realized “if I can’t be a slave to anything then I’ll be a slave to the biggest thing there is” the best thing about this is that in a sense you could think about it being a slave to one thing (G-d) or nothing but in reality it’s the same thing

what gods?

  • title is supposed to reference what people are thinking when they see this essay specifically atheists

the religion with no God

  • atheism
  • people believing only in science which doesn’t make sense because science is a tool for understanding the world unlike religion which is for living in the world

why we need to return

  • nihilism / lack of meaning and purpose
  • we have a sense of pride and thinking that we know everything when in reality we just scratched the surface and know nothing

item.title: the psychology behind mitzvot and Judaism
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • find psychological meaning in each mitzvah and what they were meant / symbolized
  • maybe add some philosophical pinpoints on certain mitzvot

item.title: twin competition
Why smart people believe stupid things
item.description:
Bullet points:

  • working inside hierarchies
  • emotional thinking versus rational thinking (reference thinking fast and slow)
  • just because you’re smart doesn’t mean you’re immune to toxic empathy
  • examples: same sex partners for better with children, as propaganda and false
  • “excuses” for being different
  • do they just not know any better?
  • we need to start asking the hard questions
  • https://youtu.be/1Uam5ulqn4g

I don’t like to throw around the word stupid because paradoxically it makes you sound stupid So let me clarify:

item.title: Wokeism is the the Tower of Babel story all over again
